key_points: The environment includes all living and non-living things around us
An ecosystem is a system where living organisms interact with their surroundings
Living components of an ecosystem are called biotic components
Non-living components such as air, water and soil are called abiotic components
Green plants are called producers because they prepare their own food
Animals are consumers because they depend on other organisms for food
Decomposers break down dead plants and animals and recycle nutrients
examples: Food chain example: Grass → Grasshopper → Frog → Snake
Plants like grass and trees are producers
Animals like cows and deer are herbivores
Bacteria and fungi act as decomposers in nature
formulas: Food chain → A sequence where one organism eats another
Food web → A network of many connected food chains
Biodegradable substances → Materials that can be broken down by microorganisms
Non-biodegradable substances → Materials that cannot be easily decomposed
exam_tips: Learn the difference between biotic and abiotic components
Know the roles of producers, consumers and decomposers
Practice simple examples of food chains
